text: The patient is positioned in the prone jackknife position with the buttocks taped aside. Surgical retractors are being placed to expose the anal region for the procedure.
The surgeon probes the blind high intersphincteric fistula tract through the internal opening, observing several milliliters of purulent discharge. A swab is taken for microbiological analysis.
The surgeon performs a phthalotomy using monopolar electrocautery, with a probe placed from the internal opening to the end of the blind intersphincteric fistula tract.
The surgeon performs curettage on the open fistula tract, removing debris and tissue from the exposed intersphincteric fistula.
The surgeon performs marsupialization on the fistula tract, creating an open pouch by suturing the edges of the tract to the surrounding skin.
The surgeon inserts a hemostatic sponge into the surgical site, likely to control bleeding within the exposed anal region.
